definition: The standard fivefold framework of active distractions that obstruct collected practice.
notes: This collective entry should inherit the headword policy for nīvaraṇa. Use five distractions as the house default and keep five hindrances only as the controlled alternate for comparative prose.

translation_policy.default_scope: Use this as the standard collective label for the five-distraction framework in governed practice and jhana-entry contexts.
translation_policy.when_not_to_apply: Do not fall back to hindrances as an unmarked default unless comparative prose explicitly needs it, and do not replace the collective label with ad hoc problem-language.
translation_policy.drift_risk: Prevents the governed five-distraction framework from drifting back into mixed hindrance and generic obstacle language.
